HomePage.title: Commands
HomePage.subtitle: All pnpm commands available from the repo root.
HomePage.newAppDescription: Interactive wizard that scaffolds a full Next.js PWA — i18n, optional auth, optional PWA, Dockerfile, and a Helm chart — ready to deploy.
HomePage.newApiDescription: Interactive wizard that scaffolds a Django REST API with your choice of database, optional Redis, email, and passkeys. Creates the app directory, Dockerfile, and virtualenv.
HomePage.generateIconsDescription: Generates PWA icons (192×192, 512×512, maskable) and favicon.ico from your app logo using FFmpeg. Run after placing a logo in your app's public folder.
HomePage.secretsDescription: Creates or patches a Kubernetes Secret from your app's env.example. Prompts you to choose which variables to include and enter their values.
HomePage.deployAppDescription: Full deployment pipeline: bumps the version, builds and pushes a Docker image, and deploys via Helm. Validates that required secrets exist before building. Pass -y to skip confirmations in CI.
HomePage.helmDescription: Interactively manage a Helm release: check status, reveal secrets, deploy, force redeploy, restart rollout, rollback, or uninstall.
HomePage.deployServicesDescription: Deploy a managed database or cache to Kubernetes via Helm. Each command walks you through namespace, credentials, and persistent storage size.
HomePage.djangoSuperuserDescription: Creates a Django superuser in a running production pod via kubectl exec. Discovers pods automatically and prompts for username, email, and password.
HomePage.logsDescription: Stream or snapshot logs from one or more Kubernetes pods. Select pods interactively, configure tail size, and choose between live and snapshot mode.
HomePage.devDescription: Start apps in development mode with hot reload.
HomePage.buildDescription: Compile packages and apps for production.
HomePage.lintDescription: Run static analysis, type checks, and formatting.
HomePage.gettingStartedSubtitle: Follow these steps to set up your local development environment.
HomePage.prereqDescription: Make sure you have all of these before you begin.
HomePage.prereqGitNote: installed automatically by the setup script
HomePage.cloneRepoDescription: Clone the repository to your local machine.
HomePage.setupScriptDescription: The interactive script checks and installs all required tools: Git, Node.js, pnpm, Docker, kubectl, Helm, Python, Django, and Claude Code. It also creates an SSH key if you don't have one.
HomePage.sshKeyDescription: After the setup script runs, your SSH public key will be displayed. Share it with the cluster admin or open an issue on GitHub to request cluster access.
HomePage.verifyDescription: Confirm that kubectl and Helm are working and that your kubeconfig is connected to the cluster.
HomePage.servicesSubtitle: Live internal services and their API endpoints.
HomePage.devCycleSaveDescription: Stage all changes and commit with a message.
HomePage.devCyclePushDescription: Push your committed changes to the remote repository.
HomePage.devCyclePullDescription: Fetch and merge the latest changes from the remote repository into your current branch.
HomePage.devCycleUndoDescription: Revert all uncommitted changes in the working tree. This cannot be undone.
HomePage.devCycleNewBranchDescription: Create a new branch and switch to it immediately.
HomePage.devCycleSwitchMainDescription: Switch back to the main branch.
HomePage.devCycleSecretsDescription: Creates or patches a Kubernetes Secret from your app's env.example. Prompts you to choose which variables to include and enter their values.
HomePage.devCycleLogsDescription: Stream or snapshot logs from one or more Kubernetes pods. Select pods interactively, configure tail size, and choose between live and snapshot mode.
HomePage.devCycleRevealSecretsDescription: Interactively manage a Helm release. Select 'Reveal secrets' from the operations menu to decode and print Kubernetes secret values.
HomePage.devCycleDeployDescription: Full deployment pipeline: bumps the version, builds and pushes a Docker image, and deploys via Helm. Validates that required secrets exist before building.
HomePage.toolsEditVideosInvokeDescription: Run from the repo root. The script is fully interactive — no command-line flags are required.
HomePage.toolsEvFlowDescription: The script guides you through: language selection → FFmpeg check (auto-downloads a static BtbN build if missing) → GPU detection → input folder → format filter → output folder → skip-already-processed check → action selection → codec choice (H.264 / H.265) → per-action parameter prompts → pre-flight summary → optional background mode.
HomePage.toolsEvActionsDescription: Select any combination of the 14 actions at the checkbox menu. Actions are applied in the order listed below.
HomePage.toolsEvBlackBarsDesc: Runs FFmpeg cropdetect on each file and applies a crop filter to remove letterbox and pillarbox bars. Fully automatic — no parameters.
HomePage.toolsEvFpsDesc: Doubles (or quadruples / octuples) the frame rate using FFmpeg's minterpolate filter with motion-compensated interpolation. CPU-only; expect slower processing on long files.
HomePage.toolsEvH264Desc: Re-encodes the video stream to H.264 using the best available encoder — NVENC (NVIDIA), VAAPI (AMD/Intel), or libx264 as software fallback. Audio is copied without re-encoding.
HomePage.toolsEvStabDesc: Two-pass video stabilization via vid.stab (vidstabdetect + vidstabtransform). Falls back to FFmpeg deshake if vid.stab is unavailable. Offers Standard and Concert presets, or fully custom parameters.
HomePage.toolsEvDenoiseDesc: Applies the hqdn3d filter (high-quality 3D denoiser) to reduce temporal and spatial noise. Higher values remove more noise at the cost of fine detail.
HomePage.toolsEvSharpenDesc: Applies an unsharp mask. Matrix size controls the kernel radius; luma amount controls luminance sharpening strength; chroma amount controls color sharpening (typically left at 0).
HomePage.toolsEvUpscaleDesc: Scales the video up to a target resolution using the Lanczos algorithm, preserving aspect ratio. Automatically skipped if the source already meets or exceeds the target.
HomePage.toolsEvDownsizeDesc: Scales the video down to a target resolution using Lanczos. Automatically skipped if the source is already at or below the target.
HomePage.toolsEvColorDesc: Adjusts contrast, brightness, saturation, and gamma via FFmpeg's eq filter. All parameters default to neutral values and are applied in a single pass.
HomePage.toolsEvMpgDesc: Converts legacy .mpg / .mpeg files to H.264 MP4 while preserving quality. Useful for processing old camcorder footage that RIFE or Video2X cannot handle as raw MPEG streams.
HomePage.toolsEvRifeDesc: AI-powered frame interpolation via rife-ncnn-vulkan. Produces smoother motion than minterpolate at the cost of longer processing time. Requires a Vulkan-capable GPU. The binary and model are downloaded automatically on first use.
HomePage.toolsEvVideo2xDesc: AI upscaling via Real-ESRGAN (realesrgan-ncnn-vulkan). Produces sharper results than Lanczos at the cost of longer processing time. Requires a Vulkan-compatible GPU. Downloaded automatically on first use.
HomePage.toolsEvDeep3dDesc: AI stabilization using optical flow computed by a PyTorch/CUDA pipeline. Requires a CUDA-capable NVIDIA GPU. Python dependencies are installed automatically in a local virtualenv on first use.
HomePage.toolsEvTiktokDesc: Full AI-assisted reel pipeline: samples frames, scores them with a local Ollama multimodal LLM, selects the best clips, smart-crops to 9:16 (1080×1920), normalizes audio at 30 fps, concatenates into a single reel, and optionally mixes in background music. Requires Ollama running locally (ollama serve).
HomePage.toolsEvScriptDesc: Interactive CLI batch video editor. Scans a folder for video files, presents a checkbox menu of processing actions, and re-encodes outputs into a dated subfolder. Most actions use FFmpeg filters; AI actions (RIFE, Video2X, Deep3D) require a compatible GPU and are downloaded automatically on first use.
HomePage.toolsDcDescription: Scans Docker or MicroK8s containerd images and presents an interactive checkbox menu to prune unused resources. Supports --dry-run mode to preview all removals without deleting anything.
HomePage.toolsDcInvokeDescription: Supports --dry-run (preview only, no deletions) and --no-color (plain output for logs). Both flags can be combined.
HomePage.toolsDcFlowDescription: language → dry-run mode → runtime selection (docker / microk8s, auto-detected if only one is present) → scan images, stopped containers, and build cache → select operations → age threshold prompt (only if removing old images) → confirmation → execute → after-cleanup disk usage summary.
HomePage.toolsDcDanglingDesc: Removes untagged images — intermediate build layers left behind after a newer image was built. Maps to docker image prune -f.
HomePage.toolsDcOldHeading: Remove unused images older than N days
HomePage.toolsDcOldDesc: Removes non-in-use images older than a configurable age threshold (default: 10 days). Images with unknown creation dates are excluded. The threshold is only prompted when this operation is selected.
HomePage.toolsDcAllUnusedDesc: Removes every image not referenced by any running or stopped container. Maps to docker image prune -a -f.
HomePage.toolsDcStoppedDesc: Removes containers with exited or dead status. Maps to docker container prune -f. Docker only.
HomePage.toolsDcCacheDesc: Clears the Docker BuildKit build cache. Maps to docker builder prune -f. Docker only.
HomePage.toolsDcSystemPruneDesc: Removes all stopped containers, dangling images, unused networks, and build cache in one operation. Maps to docker system prune -f. Docker only.
HomePage.toolsPvDescription: Media player for Ubuntu Server via HDMI using DRM/KMS output — no desktop required. Wraps mpv with DRM/KMS video output and ALSA audio. Supports files, directories, and playlists. Automatically detects audio-only vs. video content.
HomePage.toolsPvInvokeDescription: Requires mpv (apt install mpv). Provide a file path, directory, or playlist file as the positional argument.
HomePage.toolsPvMediaDesc: Specify a playlist file or force audio-only mode (auto-detected from file extension by default).
HomePage.toolsPvPlaybackDesc: Control looping, shuffle, and fullscreen behavior. --loop and --loop-playlist default to infinite when provided without a value.
HomePage.toolsPvDisplayDesc: Configure the DRM connector, display mode, device path, and mpv profile. Run --list-connectors to discover available connectors and supported resolutions.
HomePage.toolsPvAudioDesc: Set volume, audio output driver, and audio device. Run --list-audio-devices to discover available ALSA devices and their device strings.
HomePage.toolsPvAdvancedDesc: All arguments after -- are forwarded directly to mpv, giving access to the full mpv option set.
HomePage.toolsSaDescription: Security and performance audit for Ubuntu Server running MicroK8s, Plex Media Server, and Samba. Outputs a color-coded terminal report and a severity-sorted summary with actionable mitigation commands for every finding. Requires sudo.
HomePage.toolsSaInvokeDescription: Must run as root (sudo). Pass --no-color to disable ANSI output for plain-text logging or CI pipelines.
HomePage.toolsSaChecksDescription: The audit runs 12 checks in sequence and categorizes findings by severity: CRITICAL, HIGH, MEDIUM, LOW, INFO. A sorted summary with mitigation commands is printed at the end.
HomePage.scraperIntro: Headless-browser microservice powered by Playwright. Exposes web search and content extraction via JSON endpoints at scraper.iguzman.com.mx, all protected by an X-API-Key header.
HomePage.scraperHealthDescription: Health check. Returns '{ status: "ok" }'. No authentication required.
HomePage.scraperSearchDescription: Run a web search and return structured results. Supports DuckDuckGo (default) and Bing engines, with 1-20 results per request.
HomePage.scraperExtractDescription: Navigate to a URL and return its readable text content. Strips nav, footer, ads, and other boilerplate.
HomePage.scraperErrorNetwork: Network error — could not reach the scraper service.
HomePage.diarizationIntro: Speaker diarization and transcription microservice powered by pyannote.audio and faster-whisper. Upload an audio or video file and receive speaker-attributed segments at diarization.iguzman.com.mx.
HomePage.diarizationHealthDescription: Health check. Returns '{ status: "ok" }'. No authentication required.
HomePage.diarizationDiarizeDescription: Upload an audio or video file and receive a list of speaker segments with start/end timestamps. Optionally constrain the speaker count for better accuracy.
HomePage.diarizationTranscribeDescription: Upload an audio or video file and receive a speaker-attributed transcript. Runs diarization and transcription in parallel. Each segment contains speaker label, timestamps, and transcribed text.
HomePage.diarizationErrorNetwork: Network error — could not reach the diarization service.
